title: Subitem timeline in item timeline (without mirroring)
excerpt: Hello, I found an option to reflect subitems timeline by mirroring subitem timeline, however I have items which don’t have subitems, so I wanted to check if there is any option to show subitems timeline directly in item …

posts.author: IlonaK (Ilona Kaganovich)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello, I found an option to reflect subitems timeline by mirroring subitem timeline, however I have items which don’t have subitems, so I wanted to check if there is any option to show subitems timeline directly in item timeline, without mirroring?
I think it will be much better.
Thanks for answering!

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @IlonaK

Are you looking to get the overall timeline of all your subitems into the parent item? If you use the standard “Show summary in parent” there are a few things to know.

The parent column that gets created on “Show summary in parent”:

  • is a special kind of mirror and therefore can’t be used in formulas and automations
  • when there are no subitems you can’t enter data into that parent item column

The app “Rollup Subitems” (see https://monday.com/marketplace/10000124) does solve both of these issues (and many more )

posts.author: IlonaK (Ilona Kaganovich)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Bas,
Thank you for response, what I mean is that I don’t want to see additional column in the item row, what I expected is to see item timeline updated accordingly to subitems timeline changes.
Please see screenshot from my board

Timeline_vs subitem timeline1057×320 46.9 KB

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
That is exactly what the app is doing

image2146×836 193 KB

posts.author: IlonaK (Ilona Kaganovich)
posts.cookedHtml:
Perhaps I explained myself not so clearly:)
Assuming you did the following steps:
Click 3 dots in the right corner of subitem timeline-> choose “Show summary on Parent Item”
If yes, I done it already and it’s not what I wanted. Since most of my items don’t have subitems I want to see only 1 Timeline column that reflect dates of the items and subitems (where applicable)
When I’m choosing this option “Show summary on Parent Item” I see 2 Timeline columns- 1 for item and the second for subitems.
Maybe I missed something?

posts.author: Marius.Manic (Marius)
posts.cookedHtml: I would recommend, for the consistency of the database, to have at least one subitem for each item.

posts.author: IlonaK (Ilona Kaganovich)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for idea, will think about if won’t find another options:)

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @IlonaK

The app I explained above does not use the " Show summary on Parent Item" at all. The app has its own mechanism to get the data from the subitems into a real monday timeline column in the parent item. Therefore the same timeline column can be used when you have items that doesn’t have subitems.

posts.author: IlonaK (Ilona Kaganovich)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Bas,
Thank you for explanation, I’m not surer that my company would purchase additional app, maybe you know how to fix this problem directly from monday.com?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @IlonaK

What you want is not possible with out-of-the-box monday.com. There are many area where apps extends the possibilities of monday.com. If your company does not allow app, you are limited to the functionality monday.com gives you.

posts.author: IlonaK (Ilona Kaganovich)
posts.cookedHtml: Ok, thank you Bas, you helped me to understood this issue.

Wow! Thank you so much.
I tried now to use it on timeline, but it seems the automation doesn’t summarize it to the parent task. What am I doing wrong?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Looks like you didn’t follow the HowTo document, see https://excellent-team.nl/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Rollup-Subitems.pdf and didn’t specify the prerequisite triggers.

image1256×829 132 KB

posts.author: christinar (Christina Rozen)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for the explanation. I would suggest making it more intuitively. There was no place telling me to read the HowTo doc or that there is one…

posts.author: MrMilbe (Chris Von)
posts.cookedHtml: Just stumbled over the same limitation. Parent/Child timeline synch is such an obvious and mandatory requirement for a PM tool. That’s like MS starting to offer Word without letters and asking users to purchase them via 3rd party apps. Are those 1.6k for unlimited boards per user ? Per workspace ? How does the limited amount of boards version work ? Can I apply the feature to X amount of boards at a time and move them around or do they get used up ( lets say I create a board and delete it later) ?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml: The Rollup Subitems app is licensed per board unrelated to users / workspaces etc. When you delete the integrations from one board that license is “freed Up” and can be used for another board. As long as the total number of boards where you installed the app is lower or equal to the number of boards purchased you are fine.

posts.author: MrMilbe (Chris Von)
posts.cookedHtml: I gave up. For everyone else with that problem, look into Smartsheets. That does it out of the box, has unlimited subitem hierarchies and no dependency limitations. I had the unthankful job to evaluate different PM tools for my company and spend the whole weekend trying to wrestle Monday, Asana and ClickUp into submission. I think they are just made for a different use case, managing large teams with simple repetitive tasks. If you want something MS Project like for an engineering project with hundreds of dependencies and complex task hierarchies all of them fail.

posts.author: christinar (Christina Rozen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi again, I’m trying to understand, please help me:
In my company (Nayax) we have many Monday users. All of them set on the company account, but act as separate accounts. When we purchase your add-on, and we go for the Unlimited Target Boards do we need to purchase it for each one of the users or we purchase it once for the whole company?
Thanks

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @christinar

The app will work within a specific monday account (monday account URL). So, if you have just one monday account URL (like mycompany-team.monday.com) the app will work for all users in that account.

posts.author: workflowboost (Bartosz @ Workflow Boost)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

This automation is now available in our Subitems Pro Automations app:

image363×276 8.45 KB

With this recipe you can rollup (aggregate) the subitems date or timeline column values to parent item date or timeline column.

There are three operators available: date range, earliest date and latest date.

image1292×374 18.6 KB

If you have any questions or requests for custom automations, feel free to reach out to us!
